methods.method_name: X-ray Diffraction Analysis of Tripoli Rock
methods.description: Process for analyzing the mineralogical composition of Tripoli rock using X-ray diffraction (XRPD) to identify its mineralogical assemblage.

steps.description: Tripoli was analysed by X-ray diffraction (XRPD) with a Siemens D5000 operating with a Bragg-Brentano geometry; CuKα = 1.518 Å, 40 kV, 40 mA, 2-35° scanning interval, step size 0.020° 2θ with a scan rate of 13 sec/step.
steps.searchable_summary: perform X-ray diffraction analysis on Tripoli rock

methods.description: Chemical analysis of Tripoli rock using X-ray fluorescence to determine its chemical composition.

steps.description: Chemical analysis of Tripoli rock is reported in Table 1 and was performed by X-ray fluorescence analysis (Axios-Max Advanced Panalytical; 60KV; 160 mA; 4000 W; 0.0001°2 θ).
steps.searchable_summary: perform X-ray fluorescence analysis on Tripoli rock

methods.description: Procedure for synthesizing leucite by mixing silicate and aluminate solutions and treating the Tripoli material.

steps.description: 5.19 g of the ground and powdered Tripoli material were treated with HNO3 (65%), in order to dissolve the calcite fraction.
steps.searchable_summary: treat Tripoli material with HNO3 to dissolve calcite

steps.description: The diatomitic sample (ca. 5 g after the HNO3 treatment) was added to 50 mL of KOH (6.8%). This solution was thoroughly mixed with a magnetic stirrer for 2 h and then put in a teflon reactor/bomb and heated in an oven at 80 °C for 24 h.
steps.searchable_summary: mix treated Tripoli with KOH and heat in oven

steps.description: After filtration, the remnant solid and insoluble fraction, which consisted of clay minerals and quartz, was separated from the silicate solution.
steps.searchable_summary: filter and separate insoluble fraction from silicate solution

methods.description: Procedure for synthesizing gel precursors through hydrothermal treatment and subsequent calcination.

steps.description: The reactants were vigorously mixed for two hours with a magnetic stirrer.
Each mixture was heated inside an autoclave at 150 °C and ambient pressure for a duration of one hour.
The hydrothermally derived gel precursors were recovered from the reactors, filtered from the solution, thoroughly washed with distilled water and dried in an oven at 40 °C for 24 hours.
steps.searchable_summary: recover, filter, wash, and dry gel precursors

steps.description: The three gel precursors were then calcined at 1000 °C with periodic sampling carried out at scheduled intervals.

methods.description: Analysis of synthesized powders to estimate the amounts of crystalline and amorphous phases using XRPD and QPA.

steps.description: All intermediate and final products of the three syntheses were analysed by XRPD under the same operating conditions as those for the 'Tripoli rock' analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: perform XRPD analysis on synthesis products

steps.description: The amounts of both the crystalline and amorphous phases in the synthesis powders were estimated using Quantitative Phase Analysis (QPA) applying the combined Rietveld and Reference Intensity Ratio (RIR) methods.

methods.description: Analysis of synthesized powders using inductively coupled plasma optical emission spectroscopy (ICP-OES) after alkaline fusion.

steps.description: Analysis of synthesized powders was performed by inductively coupled plasma optical emission spectroscopy (ICP-OES, Perkin Elmer Optima 3200 RL) after alkaline fusion of the sample in a Pt crucible (lithium meta-tetra borate pearls, at 3/2 ratio) and subsequent acid solubilization.
steps.searchable_summary: perform ICP-OES analysis on synthesized powders

methods.description: Scanning electron microscope (SEM) analysis of synthesized products to examine their microstructure.

steps.description: Scanning electron microscope (SEM) analyses were carried out with a JEOL JSM-840 with operating conditions of 15 kV and window conditions ranging from 18 to 22 mm.
steps.searchable_summary: perform SEM analysis on synthesized products

methods.description: Vibrational spectra of synthesized products obtained using an Infrared spectrometer to study their molecular structure.

steps.description: Vibrational spectra of the synthesized products were obtained with an Infrared spectrometer FTLA2000, equipped with SiC (Globar) filament source, KBr beamsplitter and DTGS detector.
steps.searchable_summary: obtain vibrational spectra using infrared spectrometer

methods.description: Study of thermal behavior of gel precursors using differential thermal analysis and thermogravimetry (DTA-TG).

steps.description: Thermal behaviour of gel precursors were studied by differential thermal analysis and thermogravimetry (DTA-TG) by means of a Mettler TGA/SDTA851 e instrument (10°/minute from 30° to 1100 °C, using an approximate sample weight of 10 mg in Al2O3 crucible).
steps.searchable_summary: perform DTA-TG analysis on gel precursors

methods.description: Measurement of leucite density using He-picnometry.

steps.description: Density of leucite was measured by He-picnometry using an AccuPyc 1330 pycnometer.
